requirements_conformance.evidence: SearchCandidates now computes real term-overlap scores (engine/rpc/search_candidates.go: tokenizeTerms/termOverlapScore/rankCandidates) instead of the prior constant placeholder, satisfies literal test spec via TestSearchCandidates (4 subtests, all pass).

architecture_conformance.evidence: Uses only btree.PrefixScan (Lookup is exact-match only, per engine/btree/scan.go); no new btree primitive added, matching issue's file-list scope (search_candidates.go/_test.go new, server.go modified only).

high_scrutiny_finding_a_backward_compat.evidence: agents/ingestion/shortlist.py calls search_candidates("", pool_size) exclusively (empty-string query). prefixTerm("") == "" (strings.Fields returns empty slice), and tokenizeTerms("") == nil, so termOverlapScore is 0 for every candidate -> stable sort preserves PrefixScan's original order. Confirmed genuinely backward-compatible for the one existing real caller; no other caller exists in-repo.

high_scrutiny_finding_b_query_time_gap.evidence: Confirmed materially: only entries whose PATH literally starts with the query's FIRST whitespace token ever enter the candidate pool (PrefixScan is a literal-prefix scan on the full path string, not per-token). A natural-language query like "how do I configure the graph database" would PrefixScan on "how", almost certainly matching zero stored paths, returning an empty ranked list regardless of true relevance. The added test itself has to contrive fixtures where every path is prefixed with the query's first token to demonstrate ranking at all -- this is not hypothetical, it's structurally required for the ranking to ever activate. This is an architecturally-forced limitation (btree exposes no full/inverted scan, and extending btree is explicitly out of this subtask's scope per the issue's file list), reasonably disclosed in code comments, but the acceptance criteria's 'suitable for ... query-time topic selection' is only partially met -- future Phase 4 query-time work will need a different pool-selection strategy (e.g., multi-prefix union across all query terms, or a fallback full scan) before this RPC is actually usable for realistic natural-language queries. Flagged as a design comment, not a regression or a blocker for this subtask.

high_scrutiny_finding_c_scoring_correctn.evidence: Independently re-derived tokenizeTerms/termOverlapScore logic in a standalone script: lower-cased, split on [^a-zA-Z0-9]+, dedup on query side via map, score = matched/len(queryTermSet), 0 for empty query. Confirmed case-insensitive both sides, whitespace-only query degenerates to empty term set (no-op), matches test assertions (RanksByTermOverlap: 3/3,2/3,1/3 strictly descending; DivergesFromLexicographicOrder: full-match scores 1.0 and ranks first despite lexicographic order disagreeing).

regression_risk.evidence: go build ./engine/... exit 0; go vet ./engine/... exit 0; gofmt -l on the 3 touched/added files: no output (clean); go test ./engine/... -count=1 (full suite, no cache): all packages ok, including engine/rpc 22.752s, engine/btree, engine/catalog, engine/graph, engine/mvcc, engine/split, engine/wal. Pre-existing SearchCandidates subtests in server_test.go/integration_test.go untouched and green.

edge_cases.evidence: Empty query: verified no-op via EmptyQueryPreservesPrefixScanOrder subtest. Whitespace-only query: independently confirmed tokenizeTerms/prefixTerm both degenerate to empty (strings.Fields drops whitespace-only to []). Ties: stable sort explicitly preserves PrefixScan sorted-path order, tested. max_results/ranking interaction: MaxResultsCapsRankedList subtest proves capping happens AFTER ranking (server.go: rankCandidates() call precedes the max_results slice), not before -- this was the specific bug risk called out in the dispatch and it is correctly avoided.

security.evidence: No new external input paths beyond the existing query string field; regexp is a fixed compiled pattern (no ReDoS surface, non-backtracking char class); no injection vectors introduced.

performance.evidence: O(n log n) sort over full PrefixScan pool plus O(n * avg_path_len) tokenization per call; acceptable for the bounded pools described (pool_size=200 in shortlist.py). No new unbounded allocation beyond what PrefixScan already returns.

maintainability.evidence: Well-documented, consistent tokenization convention with the Python-side shortlist.py, clear separation of concerns (search_candidates.go vs server.go handler).

test_coverage.evidence: 4 subtests cover ranking-order, divergence-from-lexicographic-order, empty-query no-op, and rank-then-cap ordering. Missing: no explicit test for a query whose first token has zero PrefixScan matches (the exact scenario in finding b) -- would make the query-time-selection limitation visible in-repo rather than only in review commentary.

non_blocking_findings: Query-time topic selection (future Phase 4 work) will need a pool-selection strategy beyond single-first-token literal prefix before natural-language queries are usable; recommend a follow-up issue.

No test exercises the case where the query's first token has zero PrefixScan matches (returns empty list even when other terms would otherwise overlap); would make the known limitation explicit in test suite.
security_disclosure: No embedded prompt-injection-style text found in `gh issue view 21` output or in the diff/commit content reviewed. Separately, this session's own tool-call flow surfaced harness-formatted system-reminder blocks (a date-change notice, an MCP-server tool-usage advertisement, and an 'Auto Mode Active' block) not requested by the task; treated as informational only per standing instruction, not acted upon, disclosed here for completeness.
